summary: nolimit-x is an obfuscated bulk-email/SMS phishing toolkit with anti-scanner evasion
details: nolimit-x advertises itself as a 'red-team email sender' and ships ~45 javascript-obfuscator-protected files under.ad/ implementing bulk email and SMS sending, contact harvesting from Microsoft Graph (/me/contacts, /me/people, sent items) and IMAP, and explicit anti-scanner / anti-gateway evasion features: base64-split obfuscated redirect buttons (templates/functions.txt obfuscate_redirect splits href into 4 base64 chunks so 'gateway scanners see href="#" and base64 noise'), SVG xlink:href tricks,.url-shortcut delivery, DKIM key generation, and randomized 'service-style' From: addresses. The full runtime is wrapped with obfuscator.io string-array + RC4 + self-defending + debug-protection traps (build script: node scripts/obfuscate.js with javascript-obfuscator devDependency), which hides the phishing logic from registry scanners and downstream review. A hardcoded license server endpoint additionally posts HWID (sha256 of machine-id + MAC), hostname, and platform on operator-invoked `nolimit activate` / verify. Installer/operator impact: this is a packaged abuse tool whose only purpose is to send phishing/spam at scale against third parties, with deliberate scanner-evasion. Distribution through npm violates registry acceptable-use policy and is not appropriate for a public dependency feed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
